topRisks: Dual leave systems with shared leave_requests
Payroll run ignores payroll_adjustments and attendance_payroll_impacts
Attendance correction workflow bypasses biometric proof
Multiple workflow approval engines without a unified inbox
Notifications and document APIs are duplicated
cleanupPriorities: Stabilize payroll run and integrate Bundle A pay sources
Canonicalize enterprise leave and retire legacy leave creation
Protect attendance correction audit and exception handling

conflicts: Shared leave_requests between legacy and enterprise flows
Legacy leave approvals may not generate payroll deductions
payroll_adjustments not consumed by payroll run
attendance_payroll_impacts not consumed by payroll run
Mismatch between payroll attendance analytics and payslip totals
Correction workflow records lack mandatory biometric proof
Shifts and attendance intelligence are split from payroll calculation
Separate approval engines for leave, payroll, recruitment, and performance
Pending approvals may not aggregate across domains
Role semantics differ across API families
Potential duplicate or missing delivery records
Legacy document system not unified with enterprise reporting
Inconsistent RBAC across document upload endpoints

rbacTenantAudit.summary: RBAC is generally present but inconsistent across legacy and new APIs. Tenant scoping exists but cross-tenant validation is incomplete.
highRiskAreas: Leave API mismatch between legacy and enterprise endpoint role behavior
Workflow approval route semantics differ by module
Superadmin can be both tenant operator and platform admin in different flows

phase1: Integrate Bundle A payroll sources into canonical payroll run
Consolidate or clearly segment workflow approval inboxes
Reconcile notification and document API duplication
phase2: Link performance promotion approvals to payroll/salary change workflows
Migrate legacy documents into enterprise reporting
Add explicit source-of-truth documentation for developers
Validate tenant isolation for key domains
